lesson.menu_desc: Checkpoint — Lessons 1–5 Consolidated: Achievements, What-Ifs, Speculation, Debriefs & Register — 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 — The Mid-Block Review — Putting Lessons 1 to 5 Together
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Victor Malvezi Paschotto | Aula 6 — The Mid-Block Review

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone. This is Sarah Mitchell with a short quarterly review for the LATAM team before we close the block. I want to recognize how far this region has come. Since January, the team has outperformed its benchmark, made significant headway on the new security platform, and overseen a pipeline that grew by more than thirty percent. In our last leadership call, the board confirmed that the LATAM results had exceeded the original forecast, and they acknowledged that the region had gained real traction. There were a few difficult moments, of course. Had we aligned the regional leads earlier, we would have avoided some of the friction over headcount, but the team navigated it well and reached a consensus in the end. The director mentioned that she might revisit the targets for the next block, so please keep your figures concise and your follow-ups prompt. I will circulate the full summary by email this afternoon. Thank you all for an outstanding quarter, and I look forward to the second half.

Hi Victor, it is Daniel here, just a quick voice note before your mid-block review. You have built a strong toolkit over these five lessons, so trust it. When you present your achievements, use the present perfect: we have made headway, we have outperformed the benchmark. When the board pushes back on a decision, do not be defensive. If you take their point and then put forward your data, you stay diplomatic. Remember the reported speech for the debrief: tell them what the team confirmed, what they suggested, and what they might revisit. And when you write the follow-up afterwards, keep it tactful and concise, with a hedged request rather than a blunt demand. One last thing. If they had not trusted the LATAM numbers, they would not have raised the targets, so speak with confidence. You have earned it. Good luck in there, and let me know how it goes. I would appreciate a quick update once the meeting wraps.
